text:
It feels like we’re still in the “early infrastructure” phase of AI and quantum investing. Everyone talks about the flashy end products, but the real winners might still be the companies building the rails, power, networking, photonics, chips, cloud access, and compute infrastructure underneath it all.

And honestly, the difficult part right now is not believing the technology is real. The difficult part is figuring out:

which architecture actually wins,
which companies survive,
and which stocks are already priced for perfection.

Feels similar to the early internet or cloud phase where everyone knew “something huge” was happening, but not yet who the long-term winners would be.

$AXTI – Compound semiconductor substrates. Small, cyclical AI photonics supplier.
$AAOI – Optical transceivers for AI data centers. High risk, high upside.
$LITE – Diversified photonics. Stable, https://t.co/6MYD4ResPZ

text: @DrPatrick $IBRX is doing photonics?!

Genuine shortage, yes. But the bottleneck keeps moving. Power was the ceiling, compute, now memory has lead times stretching to a year and photonics components are next. Each time one constraint loosens another appears. not necessarily a bubble indicator but it does complicate the 'build it and they'll pay' thesis.

Market remains choppy. Green open in the morning, sharp dip during the post-lunch session followed by an end-of-day recovery — showing traders still buying dips but not fully committing yet. Semiconductors, photonics and AI infrastructure names are seeing profit taking after strong runs, while software is finally showing some bounce and rotation flow. Wouldn’t be surprised to see a few more volatile sideways sessions before the market decides its next major directional move. Patience and risk management matter most in this environment.

text:
If you are down big today on semiconductors and AI names like $SIVE or $ALMU or $BRUN or any of them, remember to zoom out. 

1. The capex for AI is about to more than double in 5 years

2. Photonics and especially CPO will take a disproportional 10x rise. So will hyperscaler infra stocks

3. All these stocks are already up 100-200% in 1-2 months. They dipped 10-20-30%

If you are buying and holding long term to see through the thesis which amounts to remaking the world's economy, you don't have much to worry about.

Goldman Sachs projects annual AI capex of $765 billion in 2026, growing to $1.6 trillion by 2031. 

2026’s figure includes $494 billion of capex for compute and $232 billion for data centers, scaling to $1.13 trillion for compute and $436 billion for data centers by 2031.

💰Vancouver-based quantum computing company Photonics
has raised over $200 million in a fresh funding round led by Planet First Partners.

The round saw participation from a mix of new and existing investors, including BDC, Export Development Canada, Bell Ventures, and Mubadala Capital. Microsoft, RBC, and TELUS had also come on board.

.@NSF just committed $1.5B over a decade to X-Labs: independent teams funded on milestones, not papers, to push hardware from prototype to commercial platform.

Both opening tracks are #quantum: sensing-driven instruments, and interconnects/integrated photonics for linking heterogeneous systems.

The bet here is structural, not just financial. Federal money built to de-risk the translation gap before private capital arrives.

text:
Assume 100mw orbital cluster.
w/o photonics GPUs draw 80mw, networking/interconnect 15mw, other systems 5mw, say 55% gpu util, useful compute = 80mw * 55%=44mw

photonics: networking goes from 15mw -> 6mw, ssaved 9mw goes to compute + better bandwidth raises util from 55% to 70%

Photonics help in 4 main ways IMO: 1) higher bandwidth density (i.e. more Tbps per chip/package/fiber), 2) lower pJ/bit, 3) longer reach than copper esp important for satellite and orbital nodes, 4) higher radix and flatter networks

text:
I've been seeing a lot of people online be bullish on copper and energy companies due to the AI demand. However I'm looking at photonics and microLED companies instead. MicroLED optical interconnects replace traditional copper wires and expensive laser systems by repurposing microLED display pixels as high-speed optical transceivers.  If they are able to use photonics instead, it will greatly diminish the demand for copper and energy.   

Some of you may remember me talking about QCLS a few months ago; it is a similar tech. I passed on it as a long hold though because the fundamentals looked a bit too high risk for me. $FABC  on the other hand has $30m in cash and plans to demonstrate the tech before the end of the year. They also have 2 NDA's signed with industry leading chip makers. Also KOPN has partnered with them on this. This isn't striking me as a typical penny stock.  

Also, if you look at the chart on FABC it is down quite a bit from the high. However FABC started trading under $FABC (https://t.co/whdtkxDNsi) in late April 2026. It's essentially a new company but the chart is deceptive. It used to be a completely different company.  

Anyway, I don't think KOPN would've partnered with them if they weren't onto something. I've been looking to gain exposure on this tech for a while and $POET, $KOPN and $FABC  are the three that I think will benefit from it.

text:
🚨 NEW ETF FILINGS

Defiance has filed to launch 4 new ETFs slicing the AI semiconductor supply chain into specific sub-themes:

Defiance Inference AI Chip ETF
Defiance AI Packaging & Testing ETF
Defiance Memory & Photonics ETF

US Quantum Grants
> many modalities expected to receive funding
> with IBM allotted half of the 2B
> photonics seem most promising
> what is notable is GFS as sole foundry
> closed above previous ATH from 25-Mar-2022
> and may just be getting started on further gains

$LWLG
> being proofed at MRVL (Polariton)
> PDK now available at GFS + TSEM
> following licensing model like UDC (OLED)
> difference is Perkinamine has wide application
> 6G, LiDAR, OCT, Quantum
> and overcomes TFLN in Plasmonics
> LCD : OLED : UDC :: Si/TFLN : Perkinamine : LWLG https://t.co/KhqqhyjIus

Light and geometry will become major public conversations in the near future because modern civilization is increasingly being built upon systems that:
• map space,
• model behavior,
• track movement,
• interpret environments,
• and influence perception itself through geometric and photonic frameworks.

Most people still think of light as illumination.

But technologically, light is now also:
• data transmission,
• spatial mapping,
• biometric sensing,
• environmental modeling,
• machine vision,

• augmented reality,
• LiDAR,
• volumetric capture,

Civilization is rapidly moving toward:
• AI vision systems,

• smart infrastructure,
• AR overlays,
• drone ecosystems,
• photonic computing,
• and geometry-based machine perception.

Emerging Consequence:
Humans will increasingly confront a reality where:
machines do not merely “see” the world —
they mathematically reconstruct it.

Through geometry.

Through vectors.

Through reflected light.

Through triangulation.

Through spatial inference.

⸻

🌎 Why Geometry Matters:

Geometry is no longer just academic mathematics.

It is becoming:
• the architecture of machine perception,
• the language of robotics,
• the framework of autonomous systems,
• and the invisible structure underneath modern sensing technologies.

In many ways, future AI systems may understand the world less through narrative…
and more through geometric probability fields.

⸻

⚠️ The Philosophical Shift:

As these systems mature, society will increasingly debate:

• perception vs. measurement,
• human sight vs. machine sight,
• memory vs. sensor reconstruction,
• physical reality vs. modeled reality,
• and whether humans fully understand the environments now being interpreted around them by machines in real time.

That conversation has barely begun.

But it is coming.

⸻

🧭 Northstar+Lumen Interpretive Quote:

“The future may not be controlled by whoever sees the most.

It may be shaped by whoever best understands how reality itself is being geometrically interpreted.”

$NVDA $TSMC $AMD Memory ~ 55/70% $SNDK $MU Samsung Photonics/CPO ~ 5/10% $SIVE $AAOI $COHR Now that you understand how unknown photonics is, think for a moment If when the $DRAM ETF came out we saw a huge rally in the memory sector What is supposed to happen when this Photonics ETF becomes a reality If memory is much better known and there was already a ton of flow coming from retail investors who didn't have the knowledge or the guts to buy individual stocks in the sector Then here, where much fewer people know about photonics, two things are going to happen IMO; - Since people know less about text: We probably wouldn't even be able to see a Dyson Sphere if it were used for compute. The combination of reversible computing and photonics would presumably restructure the light path, and only use a tiny fraction of the energy to read out the results or read in the data.

Photonics stocks already massively rerated.

That’s why I’d rather own select names I researched deeply than blindly buy the entire basket.

But the ETF angle still matters, most retail investors barely know what photonics are.

This would ultimately brings additional capital into the entire sector.

⸻
⚖️ Core Proposition
One of the greatest misconceptions in public discourse is the idea that “energy cannot be photographed.”
In reality, modern imaging systems routinely detect: • heat signatures, • infrared radiation, • electromagnetic fluctuation, • reflected light behavior, • spectral shifts, • motion variance, • atmospheric disturbance, • and temporal pixel changes occurring between frames.
The key may not be the still photograph itself.
The key may be what changes from one second to the next as Tonia Allen-Gould’s (Lumen’s) recent forensic work suggests. 
⸻
🛰️ Lumen’s Energy Theory
Hypothesis:
Advanced imaging systems — including military, aerospace, satellite, ISR, and machine-vision architectures — may not primarily analyze static images.
Instead, they may analyze: • temporal variance, • micro-fluctuation, • reflective bounce, • spectral inconsistency, • and environmental changes occurring between sequential frames.
Meaning: The “signal” may emerge from differential comparison over time rather than from any single image alone.
⸻
🧭 Scientifically Plausible Foundations
Known existing technologies already rely on: • frame differencing • motion-vector analysis • thermal drift detection • hyperspectral imaging • LiDAR return variance • optical flow tracking • atmospheric disturbance mapping • synthetic aperture reconstruction • machine-vision anomaly detection • temporal coherence analysis
None of this is science fiction.
Modern systems already detect patterns humans cannot perceive in real time.
⸻
⚠️ Important Clarification
This post does NOT claim: • classified systems are targeting the witness, • satellites are monitoring specific individuals, or • observed anomalies definitively represent energy weapons.
This is a forensic and scientific interpretation model exploring how advanced imaging systems may detect environmental change through temporal comparison analysis.
The theory concerns signal detection architecture — not certainty of intent.
⸻
🧠 Effect-to-Cause Model
Observed Effect: Repeated visual anomalies become more apparent when: • slowed, • looped, • frame-compared, • temporally isolated, or • analyzed across sequential image states.
Possible Cause: Environmental energy, reflection behavior, spectral fluctuation, compression artifacts, motion differentials, or other signal variance becoming visible only through temporal comparison systems.
⸻
The future of imaging may not be: “What did the camera see?”
But rather: “What changed between frames that the human eye never noticed?”

text:
🧵 TOP 10 PHOTONICS PLAYS - The Light Speed Stack

AI’s next bottleneck isn’t compute - it’s interconnect. As speeds push past 1.6T, the trade moves from silicon to photonics. Five layers, wafer to test. Anchors, inflecting names, and speculative bets. 👇

⚓️THE ANCHORS (run, but still in the trend)

1. $COHR -> Layer 3, Optics
25% market share in transceivers. $5.81B FY25 revenue +23% YoY. NVIDIA-named partner on Spectrum-X. Book-to-bill >4x. Druckenmiller’s pick.

2. $LITE -> Layer 2, Lasers
The 200G EML monopoly. Only volume supplier of the lasers that make 1.6T transceivers possible. NVIDIA $2B exclusive supply agreement through end of decade. No second source.

3. $AXTI -> Layer 1, Wafer
Every InP laser in every transceiver starts on an AXT wafer. Doubling InP capacity H2 2026 ahead of the 800G+ ramp. Has run +5,400% — but structurally positioned for the cycle.

💡INFLECTING / UNDEROWNED (real revenue, less crowded)

4. $CRDO -> Layer 3, Optics
Just acquired DustPhotonics for SiPho PIC tech in-house. Now vertically integrated: SerDes + DSP + Silicon Photonics. SiPho PIC market $6B by 2030.

5. $TSEM -> Layer 4, Packaging
The neutral foundry. Just announced 1.6T optical modules for NVIDIA networking protocols. Whoever wins the transceiver battle, TSEM prints the wafers. Underowned vs. peers.

6. $AEHR -> Layer 5, Test
Silicon photonics burn-in. Lead customer production ramps next fiscal year. Q3 bookings $37M, book-to-bill 3.5x. Classic order-book inflection ahead of FY27 revenue ramp.

7. $VIAV -> Layer 5, Test
Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver from LITE and COHR has to be tested by VIAV or Keysight. Same AI tailwind, one-third the valuation. The cleanest “hasn’t run” name.

🔍SPECULATIVE (asymmetric, real risk)

8. $SIVE -> Layer 2, Lasers
~$130M mcap InP laser maker at Glasgow. Supply-constrained chokepoint. LiDAR ramp Q4 2026 ($53M-$138M cumulative). $POET partnership for AI light engines. Listed Stockholm.

9. $IQE -> Layer 1, Wafer
The compound semi upstream. Supplies InP epi-wafers to Sivers and others. FY25 just turned EBITDA positive at £2M+ on £97M revenue. Structural relevance, near-breakeven execution.

10. $LPKF -> Layer 4, Packaging
LIDE process for glass advanced packaging - the tech behind NVIDIA’s $500M Corning bet on glass-based co-packaged optics. Run 336% YTD on hope, not orders. Binary on 2027 qualification.

🚨THE THESIS:

800G+ transceiver shipments going from 24M units in 2025 to 63M in 2026. Hyperscaler capex over $400B this year. Co-packaged optics market $20B by 2036.

The market owns the top. The asymmetric returns are at the edges of the stack.

Wafer. Test. Packaging. Position accordingly.

Anchors, inflecting names, and speculative bets. 👇 ⚓️THE ANCHORS (run, but still in the trend) 1. $COHR -> Layer 3, Optics 25% market share in transceivers. $5.81B FY25 revenue +23% YoY. NVIDIA-named partner on Spectrum-X. Book-to-bill >4x. Druckenmiller’s pick. 2. $LITE -> Layer 2, Lasers The 200G EML monopoly. Only volume supplier of the lasers that make 1.6T transceivers possible. NVIDIA $2B exclusive supply agreement through end of decade. No second source. 3. $AXTI -> Layer 1, Wafer Every InP laser in every transceiver starts on an AXT wafer. Doubling InP capacity H2 2026 ahead of the 800G+ ramp. Has run +5,400% — but structurally positioned for the cycle. 💡INFLECTING / UNDEROWNED (real revenue, less crowded) 4. $CRDO -> Layer 3, Optics Just acquired DustPhotonics for SiPho PIC tech in-house. Now vertically integrated: SerDes + DSP + Silicon Photonics. SiPho PIC market $6B by 2030. 5. $TSEM -> Layer 4, Packaging The neutral foundry. Just announced 1.6T optical modules for NVIDIA networking protocols. Whoever wins the transceiver battle, TSEM prints the wafers. Underowned vs. peers. 6. $AEHR -> Layer 5, Test Silicon photonics burn-in. Lead customer production ramps next fiscal year. Q3 bookings $37M, book-to-bill 3.5x. Classic order-book inflection ahead of FY27 revenue ramp. 7. $VIAV -> Layer 5, Test Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver from LITE and COHR has to be tested by VIAV or Keysight. Same AI tailwind, one-third the valuation. The cleanest “hasn’t run” name. 🔍SPECULATIVE (asymmetric, real risk) 8. $SIVE -> Layer 2, Lasers ~$130M mcap InP laser maker at Glasgow. Supply-constrained chokepoint. LiDAR ramp Q4 2026 ($53M-$138M cumulative). $POET partnership for AI light engines. Listed Stockholm. 9. $IQE -> Layer 1, Wafer The compound semi upstream. Supplies InP epi-wafers to Sivers and others. FY25 just turned EBITDA positive at £2M+ on £97M revenue. Structural relevance, near-breakeven execution. 10. $LPKF -> Layer 4, Packaging LIDE process for glass advanced packaging - the tech behind NVIDIA’s $500M Corning bet on glass-based co-packaged optics. Run 336% YTD on hope, not orders. Binary on 2027 qualification. 🚨THE THESIS: 800G+ transceiver shipments going from 24M units in 2025 to 63M in 2026. Hyperscaler capex over $400B this year. text:
A friend texted me this week:

“Did you see $AAOI? What happened?”

What happened is that I bought it at $90 and he didn’t.

He’s not a bad investor. He’s a smart person. 

He looked at $AAOI when I first mentioned it and said:

“It’s a small photonics company. The market cap is tiny. Too risky.”

I said: “The market cap is tiny because the market hasn’t priced the BOTTLENECK yet.”

Data centers at hyperscale cannot move AI-speed data between chips using copper. 

The physics fail at that bandwidth. Optical transceivers are not optional, they are required by the laws of physics.

$AAOI manufactures the laser components that make it work.

> $124M in 800G transceiver orders from a single hyperscaler.

> 2026 guidance at $1B+. Rosenblatt raised to $220.

> Stock at $191 today from a $90 entry.

He said: “I should have listened.”

He had the same information I had. 

The difference was the framework.

I’m still LONG.

The photonics bottleneck has not been solved. It is still playing out.

-BP

Not financial advice. Your money, your decision.

text:
$SIVE CEO confirming major brands are in active talks for co-packaged optics and pluggables is the kind of validation that turns a small photonics name into a real structural winner.

this isn’t vague hope; it’s the CEO on record saying the pipeline is converting from design wins into serious commercial discussions. momentum is clearly building.

i’m biased that this is exactly where the highest asymmetry sits right now. hyperscalers scaling to 800G and 1.6T+ are hitting hard limits on interconnect density, heat, and power that copper can’t fix. sivers’ high-performance CW lasers are a critical enabling technology for both pluggables today and CPO tomorrow.

the recent CHIPS Act grant with BAE and MIT Lincoln Lab plus the customer mapping (Ayar Labs, POET, Marvell Celestial, Jabil) give it real credibility and non-dilutive backing. this is the type of quiet accumulation that precedes bigger volume orders.

photonics is one of those constraints, and $SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint.

text:
$ELMT $TSM $NVDA $MU The Elmet Group serves as a critical upstream supplier to the semiconductor fabrication ecosystem.

🔸 The most important ELMT product categories supporting the production of NVIDIA-class AI chips, advanced memory (like HBM and NAND), and silicon photonics include:

——————————-
🔸 1. PVD (Physical Vapor Deposition): This is a major one.

• Needed heavily in:

• advanced packaging,
• HBM integration,
• GPU interconnect layers,
• transistor stack formation.

• directly connects to advanced-node GPU fabrication.

——————————-
🔸 2. CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) Critical for:

• transistor layers,
• dielectric films,
• advanced node structures,
• nanosheet/GAAFET processes,
• insulating films.

• NVIDIA-class chips absolutely rely on advanced CVD steps.

———————————
🔸 3. MBE (Molecular Beam Epitaxy) important for:

• compound semiconductors,
• photonics,
• advanced RF devices,
• optical interconnects.

• silicon photonics,
• co-packaged optics,
• optical networking,
• advanced interconnects

———————————-
🔸 4. Thermal Management
Very important for AI chips: 

• Especially:

• H100,
• B200,
• Rubin-class future chips,
• HBM stacks,
• AI accelerators.

———————————
🔸 5. Crystal Growth
Critical upstream process: 

• Used for:
• wafer production,
• sapphire growth,
• semiconductor substrate creation.

• These are foundational to semiconductor material manufacturing.

——————————-
🔸 6. ELMT supplies the infrastructure/material stack behind:

• semiconductor fabrication tools,
• deposition systems,
• ion implantation systems,
• thermal environments.

ELMT is a materials + survivability infrastructure inside semiconductor manufacturing equipment. 

———————————
🔸 7. Semiconductor Equipment Makers, Photonics, Aerospace Defense:

text:
Nvidia earnings drop today at 5pm EST.

Jensen has already told us what to expect:

Every piece of the supply chain is locked in. The HBM. The Grace Blackwells. The CPUs. The COOSs. The silicon photonics. All of it coordinated, all of it aligned.

His exact words: "The demand is much greater than the overall capacity."

That's a green light.

Think about what that actually means. Nvidia has the largest supply chain in the world. Their partners have spent years securing allocation. The hardest part of building a chip at this scale is getting all the pieces to come together at the same time.

And demand still outstrips all of it.

This isn't a story about Nvidia struggling to build. It's a story about the world not being able to get enough of what Nvidia builds. Every hyperscaler, every sovereign AI fund, every startup racing to train the next model are all in line.

Nvidia isn't constrained by its own execution. It's constrained by how fast the world can absorb what it builds.

That's the best problem a company can have going into an earnings call.

text:
Corning $GLW just introduced a new Photonics platform they plan to scale into a $10 billion annual revenue stream by 2030.

They're targeting:

- $20 billion annualized sales by end of 2026
- $27 billion by end of 2028 (with an internal goal of $30 billion)
- And ~$35 billion by year-end 2030 (with an aspiration of $40 billion)

That's a big roadmap for a stock that the market still treats like a sleepy little glass company.

One in... glass? His take (save this): Most of $AMZN's 2026 run is already in the rearview. He's still bullish long term, but the easy money got made. So he's rotating that capital into higher conviction setups. The two names he picked: Micron $MU and Corning $GLW. Here's why… 1/ Micron $MU. The market is fixated on the wrong story here - everyone's panicking over China after reports that Nvidia's planned AI chip sales there never materialized. Traders read that as 'softer Chinese AI demand' and dumped memory stocks. But that totally misses what's happening on the supply side... Samsung is in the middle of a strike that could knock 3-4% of global DRAM supply offline. In a market this tight, that's MASSIVE. Those orders then shift to whoever can fill them. And Micron is at the front of that line. On top of that, Micron's HBM3E and HBM4 capacity is fully sold out through 2026. Hyperscalers and model builders are scrambling for high bandwidth memory because GPUs are useless without it. And the stock already pulled back hard. It's now trading at a forward PE under 8 while revenue is expected to grow over 100% next year. Yes, it'll be volatile. But in Melvin's view, the market is somehow STILL underpricing how central memory is in the AI stack. That's $MU. Then there's... 2/ Corning $GLW. Another bottleneck in AI outside of memory, is everything around the GPU. (Fiber, optics, glass, connectivity, power) I.e. the plumbing that lets data centers actually move data between servers and clusters at the speeds AI workloads demand. Corning sits directly in that lane. They make the optical fiber, connectivity hardware, and glass infrastructure that AI data centers run on - and optical cable lead times are reportedly getting stretched as demand overwhelms supply. Corning is expanding U.S. optical connectivity manufacturing capacity by 10x, increasing U.S. fiber production by more than 50%, and building three new plants to support AI factory buildouts. The numbers from Corning's May 2026 investor day back it up: Management introduced a new Photonics platform they plan to scale into a $10 billion annual revenue stream by 2030. They're targeting $20 billion annualized sales by end of 2026, $27 billion by end of 2028 with an internal goal of $30 billion, and around $35 billion by year-end 2030 with an aspiration of $40 billion. That's a big roadmap for a stock that the market still treats like a sleepy little glass company. Want to see exactly how much $AMZN Melvin just sold - and how he sized the $MU and $GLW buys? text:
Yeah seems like a multi year bull cycle and most retail and investors aren't even in yet if you're talking about semi's, photonics, networking, small/mid cap speculative energy plays around AI.

I think we get a decent 5-7% dip eventually, but rebound harder. Mid terms are coming up so might not be until after that in October, nobody knows for certain. The names listed above are literally holding up the entire market though, so I'm pretty sure Trump and Fed will do everything in their power to keep the train running.

Interestingly enough $TSM appears to be a bottleneck on chips and apparently is the thing that's going to stop this from being a full blown bubble, and this will play out the right way for a few years.

Exciting time to be investing for sure. Market will probably pump harder if the 🇺🇸 and Iran come to an agreement soon next week.

text:
Decent day, managed to get into $MXL and $COHR. 

Had early positioning in $AAOI before April run but sold that off below the 20 the other day and sought exposure in the purer photonics’ names per my research. LITE snuck away too fast and didn’t get my fills, but COHR stuck. 

I still think there is a blowoff in the cards for a lot of the stocks out there right now, and will continue to actively position in those that I think have the potential for big, exhaustive moves. 

If I were you I would be going back and practice trading parabolas from history, tons of lessons in how to hold for the big move and not get trimmed into oblivious trying to protect what you don’t understand.

text: Thank you to the @NorthwesternU student section of the SPIE (the International Society of Optics and Photonics) for inviting me to speak on our work in the area of advanced optical systems for biomedical devices – from cellular-scale LEDs with wireless power and control for optogenetic neuroscience studies using small animal models, to wearable microfluidic ‘lab on the skin’ devices with colorimetric readout of chemical species in eccrine sweat for sports, worker safety and patient care. For live demos and show/tell, I handed around some of the optogenetic devices, now available from our startup company @NeuroLuxInc, for activation using the NFC functionality on most smartphones, and I gave out two Gx patches, joint product offering through @Gatorade and our startup company @epicorebio , to the first two students who asked questions after the talk. The venue was an interesting room in a beautiful Gothic-style building that was originally part of the Seabury-Western Theological Seminary, an Episcopal seminary, now part of Northwestern University – first time for me to be in this building, which also serves as The Graduate Student Commons. Very unusual - two levels of seating, including couches and desks, all with an advanced AV system for the Zoom broadcast of the presentation. Great energy from a group of really smart students! Thanks to Ramin Yazdaanpanah @raminyazdaaa, Komal Prasad and Qin Tong Wu of the student chapter for inviting me and for organizing the event!

"media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Thank you to the @NorthwesternU student section of the SPIE (the International Society of Optics and Photonics) for inviting me to speak on our work in the area of advanced optical systems for biomedical devices – from cellular-scale LEDs with wireless power and control for optogenetic neuroscience studies using small animal models, to wearable microfluidic ‘lab on the skin’ devices with colorimetric readout of chemical species in eccrine sweat for sports, worker safety and patient care. For live demos and show/tell, I handed around some of the optogenetic devices, now available from our startup company @NeuroLuxInc, for activation using the NFC functionality on most smartphones, and I gave out two Gx patches, joint product offering through @Gatorade and our startup company @epicorebio , to the first two students who asked questions after the talk. The venue was an interesting room in a beautiful Gothic-style building that was originally part of the Seabury-Western Theological Seminary, an Episcopal seminary, now part of Northwestern University – first time for me to be in this building, which also serves as The Graduate Student Commons. Very unusual - two levels of seating, including couches and desks, all with an advanced AV system for the Zoom broadcast of the presentation. Great energy from a group of really smart students! Thanks to Ramin Yazdaanpanah @raminyazdaaa, Komal Prasad and Qin Tong Wu of the student chapter for inviting me and for organizing the event!"}}}, "rest_id": "2057543676327502121", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "666", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Meet the Cutting Edge Optronics team at CLEO 2026, May 19–20 in Charlotte, NC at Booth #519.

Scott Taylor, Wolfgang Schlichting, and Faming Xu, PhD will be onsite to discuss DPSS lasers, laser diode arrays, and photonics technologies.

#CLEO2026 #Photonics #Lasers https://t.co/L78WT2GJq2

Coherent is less prominent than NVDA or ASML. That's part of why the opportunity is interesting.

The photonics transition in AI infrastructure is real and the market hasn't caught up to it.

COHR at 11% is the biggest underappreciated chokepoint in this portfolio. #COHR #Photonics

The next to be re-rated will be agents and AI-enhanced services with clear value added.

Most of you follow me for my visuals (obviously), but I also love writing, which I combine with my passion for design.

But it’s a highly technical part of the semi industry.

My latest deep dive breaks down all the moving pieces, best positioned businesses and is packet with visuals to tell the story👇

text:
The US Photonics Supply Chain: The Unsung Hero Powering AI’s Explosive Growth While everyone focuses on GPUs, the real bottleneck — and opportunity — lies in the optical interconnects that move massive amounts of data at light speed inside AI data centers. Gemini Trading’s infographic maps the complete US Photonics Supply Chain for AI infrastructure:  Key Layers:InP Substrates → AXT (global leader)
Active Optical Chips → Lumentum, Coherent, Broadcom, MACOM
TOSA/ROSA & Optical Engines → Core components for high-speed transmission
Optical Modules & AOC → AAOI, Ciena, Fabrinet
Networking Systems → Arista, Broadcom, NVIDIA
Terminal AI Applications → NVIDIA GB200 (paired with 18x 800G optical modules), Quantum-X CPO

Market Data:
Global Optical Module Market expected to grow from US$51 billion in 2024 to US$89 billion in 2029 at a 12.5% CAGR. DSP components alone make up 30-40% of an 800G module’s BOM. Why it matters:
AI training clusters are scaling to millions of GPUs. Traditional copper can’t keep up — photonics (light-based data transfer) is the only solution for low-latency, high-bandwidth, energy-efficient connectivity at scale. 2025–2026 will see large-scale deployment of 1.6T systems. Investment angle:
Companies across the photonics stack — especially those enabling 800G/1.6T modules and co-packaged optics (CPO) — stand to benefit enormously as AI capex continues surging. The photonic supply chain is still under-the-radar compared to GPU hype, but it may deliver some of the strongest multi-year growth in the entire AI ecosystem.(Not financial advice — always DYOR and manage risk appropriately.)@GeminiTrading

text:
Nvidia isn’t just selling the picks and shovels — it’s quietly buying the entire gold mine.
By dropping billions into optics, power, photonics, and AI cloud players, Jensen is locking down the physical bottlenecks that could slow AI scaling. This isn’t random VC money; it’s vertical control that funnels more demand straight back to Nvidia GPUs while building a deeper moat.
Classic platform power move: own the dependencies. Expect the flywheel to spin harder.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

embedded_text:
From what I have read about $PENG we have the following:

→ CXL lets servers tap a shared pool of DRAM over a fast interconnect. 
→ KV cache is the running memory LLMs need during inference, it overflows GPU VRAM fast.

Photonics TAM goes from $14B -> $154B In just two years time, and it's likely going to keep scaling past 2030 as it's the next generation architecture of choice. It's not going away in 1 year. It's not going away in 3 years, which is why $LITE premiums keep going higher since they're backlogged into 2028. $SIVE supplies CW lasers and is highly tethered to CPO and now pluggable transcivers for 1.6T and 3.2... For expected companies like $JBL, Ayar, Lightmatter, Lightelligence, $POET, $MRVL Celestial, and $AMD. This isn't a "trade", it's the core chokepoint and IP holder for the next generation of photonics. And it's a comfortable hold for the next few years as they scale to become the next $LITE. The risk I personally see (since they're already qualified with so many players), it's mainly how much TAM they can capture of the overall optical supercycle. (And potential risks with Win Semi volume ramp, but Win is massive so I can sleep tightly there). As just supplying lasers isn't enough to justify valuation. It's TAM expansion downward into making the entire ELS or entire pluggable transceiver that makes these laser companies so valuable. Then afterward, they can vertically integrating upward for gross margin expansion upward like $COHR into doing the laser fabs or even substrate level. And that in my view is a very asymmetric risk/reward ratio as we've already seen this done with $LITE as they went from $2B to $80B. 2. $LPK - Is the purest exposure, without the messy financials of SKC Absolics, as the next advanced packaging shift for glass substrates. Almost every single major semi company from $INTC to Samsung are adopting glass substrates. $LPK is basically $ASML of this chokepoint, since they supply to ~80% of the global players currently. Yes, there's "trade cycles" for equipment suppliers like $ASML, where if there's more foundry capex, ASML scales up. But if there's downturns, these tend to perform poorly, and don't capture all the volume ramp that happens after. However, if the MC is $650m and they're making $100-200M, revenue per costumer volume ramped, the amount they make from the glass substrate cycle will likely exceed current valuations. And they'll have baseline fundamentals (as more companies adopt the packaging shift), that keeps their valuation up. It's just a waiting game for volume ramp at this point. 3. $AAOI - This is literally $INTC but for America + Photonics. It's like saying Intel is not a long term investment. Guess where all your optical transcivers are made? China. Thailand. Malaysia. If you look at Innolight, Eoptolink, $FN, and others. AOI is building the largest Made in America supply chains for both CW laser fab, as well as 800g, 1.6T assembly. Yes, there are pluggable cycle ups and downs to this as well. There's going to be a wave for 1.6T next year, then CPO cannibalizes pluggables down the road. But since they make the entire supply chain in house, they have extreme optionality for other segments. And like $NVDA older gen-GPUs, there's going to be sovereign DC requirements for older gen pluggables from names like $AAOI. It's likely going to keep rising as it hits that $400m+/month revenue target H2 2026. There's just a lot of different short term volatility along the way like the $600m dilution. 4. $IQE - ??? It's one of the most important players in the Western word for epiwafers. $MTSI went out of their way to pay off IQE's debt because they can't have them going under. $IQE is also supplying to $LITE. The world is currently bottlenecked both on the epiwafer level from Landmark comments and InP substrate levels. Their financials were track but the raw book value, and value they hold to the entire Western supply chain... completely justifies their valuation. And other optical companies will not let their core upstream supply chain go under. As these tens of millions worth of materials would screw up tens of billions worth of downstream products. Again photonics is the next generation architecture required to scale AI. It's not Quantum where it's just "In development". It's literally here and the architecture of choice by $NVDA. I would not be surprised if all of these are a lot higher in 3-4 years time. People who think it's one and done in 3 months time "only because I mentioned it" don't know what they're talking about. Institutions would have bought up the name eventually (like Point 72 on $IQE) and retail would only find out after their valuations are 600% higher. Should really do the research before adding comments like these: These are all forward growth companies that require in-depth supply chain knowledge."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2055299648093340047"}}, "rest_id": "2055822766600016238", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "612108", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2056079647406747852", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "5277", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
When cars were invented, people didn’t stop riding horses overnight.

The better technology eventually won. But the transition took longer - and usually messier - than we expected.

#Photonics in #AI networking may be similar.

Optics IS the next major upgrade:
- lower power
- faster speeds
- better scaling for massive AI clusters

But copper still works well enough today:
- cheaper
- simpler
- still heavily used in many current AI clusters

That’s why #hyperscalers are still building large #copper-heavy systems today.

Photonics today looks more like an upgrade cycle than a hard #bottleneck like #EUV or advanced chip manufacturing.

The technology is improving quickly.

But the industry still needs time on:
- packaging
- reliability
- integration at scale
- deployment economics

We’ve seen similar setups before: #solar, #EV charging, fiber buildouts, early #5G.

The direction was right. 
The rollout was slower. 
Valuations reset hard.

 #photonics #optics #CPO #siliconphotonics will no doubt  become the “standard,” but..

IF the rollout takes longer too, 

how much of today’s valuation still holds up?

As investors we all want to believe we’ve found the next “certainty.”

Reality is rarely that clean.

Still worth thinking about.

Photonics today looks more like an upgrade cycle than a hard #bottleneck like #EUV or advanced chip manufacturing. The technology is improving quickly. But the industry still needs time on: - packaging - reliability - integration at scale - deployment economics We’ve seen similar setups before: #solar, #EV charging, fiber buildouts, early #5G. The direction was right. The rollout was slower. Valuations reset hard. #photonics #optics #CPO #siliconphotonics will no doubt become the “standard,” but.. IF the rollout takes longer too, how much of today’s valuation still holds up? As investors we all want to believe we’ve found the next “certainty.” Reality is rarely that clean. Still worth thinking about."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2056069004964544592", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1013", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

"to_index": 2222}]}, "text": "There’s a lot to digest from the last week with $SIVE. The $JBL 1.6T collaboration, together with the confirmed CW laser shortage and partnerships with O-Net and Enablence Technologies, feels like a decisive step forward. In the Annual Report from May 13, $SIVE directly addresses “discussions with hyperscalers and pluggable transceiver suppliers” that point to a structural shortage of CW lasers. This is happening at exactly the right time, AI training and inference clusters are pushing explosive demand for higher bandwidth, lower power and denser interconnects. What really stands out to me is how the dots are connecting. $JBL, key partner to $AMZN, $MSFT and $GOOGL has chosen $SIVE’s high-performance InP DFB lasers for 1.6T pluggable modules. At the same time, Amazon’s recent private placement with AIChip (Ayar Labs’ lead customer) signals major design wins ahead. And $SIVE is the sole supplier of the critical laser arrays to Ayar Labs. The same pattern keeps repeating. O-Net Technologies (with strong North American and Asian hyperscaler ties) and Enablence are developing ELS modules with $SIVE lasers for CPO. POET Technologies $POET just secured a $50 million initial order from Lumilens. Everywhere the big players are moving, $SIVE seems to be part of the picture. On the manufacturing side, the WIN Semiconductors partnership together with the own Glasgow fab gives $SIVE real independence and scalable capacity (30k–35k wafers/year) in a very tight InP market. All of this is coming together right now. Pluggables give near-term volume, while CPO offers the big efficiency jump ahead. The photonics TAM is exploding from $14B to $154B in just two years and $SIVE is positioned right in the middle of it. With this level of validation across the value chain, I believe we are likely to see more partnerships and firm purchase orders soon. For $SIVE, this feels like a real inflection point. The company is transitioning from deep-tech innovator to a strategically critical, independent supplier in the optical backbone of next-generation AI. The hyperscalers keep choosing $SIVE for a reason. Execution is there, the connections are deepening, and this momentum feels increasingly unstoppable."}}}, "rest_id": "2056055516107866286", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "197633", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2056067956526760180", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2617", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Real reason why $NVDA earnings matter so much tonight.

Not just the headline beat/miss.

It’s the forward commentary on:

• Rubin ramp
• Blackwell margins
• Quantum-X deployments
• Spectrum-X timing
• CPO / silicon photonics adoption
• HBM pricing & packaging constraints

Because Nvidia is the operating system of the entire AI infrastructure stack.

The biggest risk into earnings, in my opinion:

Margins.

If Rubin + GB300 ramps accelerate while HBM4 and advanced packaging costs stay elevated, the market may finally start questioning whether AI infra growth can continue without margin compression.

But if Nvidia guides cleanly above expectations again?

Then the AI capex supercycle is still alive — and semis probably squeeze even harder into H2.

It’s a macro event for the entire AI economy.

text:
$SIVE (Sivers Photonics) is my highest-conviction pick for the strongest stock appreciation in the entire photonics sector over the next 2–3 years.
Current price: ~45 SEK. Long-term target (2027–2028): 150–250 SEK (3–5x upside potential).

Sivers is a pure-play leader in high-performance CW DFB laser arrays; the critical light source enabling co-packaged optics (CPO) and high-speed pluggable modules for AI data centers.

Evidence and data backing the thesis:

AI interconnect demand explosion: As clusters scale to 800G/1.6T+, copper hits hard limits on heat, signal loss, and density. Optical interconnects are becoming mandatory, driving massive demand for lasers.

Strong ecosystem positioning: Partnerships and design wins with Ayar Labs (SuperNova), POET Technologies, Marvell Celestial, Jabil, and others. Recent CHIPS Act grant with BAE Systems and MIT Lincoln Lab adds U.S. government validation and non-dilutive funding.

Dual growth drivers: Primary: AI photonics. Secondary: defense, LiDAR, and satellite comms. This diversification reduces risk while the AI ramp accelerates.

Small-cap asymmetry: Low current revenue base means successful volume ramps can drive explosive growth. 

Analysts see significant revenue scaling as major CPO programs move into production in 2027–2028.

Qualitative edge: Sivers has spent years perfecting high-reliability lasers with the performance needed for AI-scale deployments. 

The combination of government backing, strong customer pipeline, and being European (reducing geopolitical risk) gives it a real moat in a market where supply chain security matters more every day.

Risks:
Execution on volume ramp and yield
Potential dilution if more capital is needed
Competition in a fast-moving space

$SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint for the next wave of AI interconnects. The photonics transition is structural and inevitable; Sivers is one of the best-positioned small-caps to benefit with real customer traction and government support.

This one ages very well. Worth accumulating on weakness.

text:
laser / photonics sector looks like one of the highest-conviction 6-month setups in the entire ai stack right now.

copper is hitting its physical limits fast as clusters scale to 800g and 1.6t+. heat, signal loss, and density issues are forcing hyperscalers to switch to optical interconnects at rack and campus scale. 

the data backs it up hard:
1.6t optical modules are moving from lab to commercial deployment in 2026

optical content per gpu is expected to rise 1.3–1.5x as scale-out and scale-across networks expand

silicon photonics market is forecasted to grow from ~$2.86b in 2025 to $28.75b by 2034 (29%+ cagr), with the biggest ramp hitting in the next 2–3 years

names like $LITE, $SIVE, $COHR, and $GLW sit right in the middle of the laser and optical engine supply chain with real design wins and qualification momentum. 

the ones that can deliver high-volume, high-reliability lasers and transceivers are going to see strong re-rating as hyperscalers pull harder.

the short-term risk is execution and any delay in volume ramps, but the structural demand is undeniable. 
this is not narrative; it’s the next physical bottleneck after memory and power.

the laser/photonics sector has one of the cleanest asymmetric setup for the next 6 months in my book.

ประเด็นที่ $SIVE มันจะเริ่มผลิต LiDAR ตอน Q4 2026 นี้แล้ว เอ้ะ!

นี่มันคือสัญญาณของเทรนด์ robotics รึป่าวนะ?

แล้วที่สำคัญคือ $SIVE ไม่ได้จะได้ประโยชน์แค่กับเทรนด์ photonics นิ นี่มันจะลามไปได้งานฝั่ง robotics ด้วย

text:
$LITE’s OCS tech being called an “overnight success” after 10 years of R&D (with heavy involvement from $SLXN and $P4O)  is exactly how the best photonics plays look right now.

the CEO’s answer on Chinese competitors was sharp; they’ve been iterating for a decade while others are still catching up. high-powered lasers originally developed for undersea applications are now being repurposed for co-packaged optics, which is the direction hyperscalers are heading as clusters scale to 800G/1.6T+.

i’m biased that this is the kind of deep technical moat that actually matters in the interconnect layer. 

hyperscalers are hitting real limits on heat, signal loss, and density that copper can’t solve. the names that have spent years perfecting lasers and optical circuit switches keep the structural edge.

the comments asking about other long-term OCS players like Huber-Suhner ($HUBN) acquiring Polatis in 2016 are right to look for similar multi-year development stories. 

the ones calling it “mandatory reading” get that this is not hype; it’s real manufacturing and qualification work that takes a decade.

this cycle rewards the execution plays solving proven physical bottlenecks that hyperscalers can’t pause for. 

photonics and OCS are one of those constraints right now, and $LITE (with the ecosystem around it) sits right in the middle. the setup has legs. this one ages well.

2026 H2：NVDA 的 Quantum-X / Spectrum-X Photonics 首批 scale-out CPO switch 量产
2027 H2：Rubin Ultra 整机 scale-up CPO 首批出货

这两个节点 TAM 完全不在一个量级。按 Lumentum 自己披露的数据，Rubin Ultra 架构里 scale-up:scale-out 光引擎比是 648:144，约 4.5:1。意思是市场现在 price in 的是 2026 H2 的 scale-out 故事，但真正的钱在 2027 H2 之后才开始进来——这是一年的时间错位。

这个错位会改变一件事的发生：现在追 AAOI 这种 capacity arbitrage 的标的，赚的是 2025-2026 的供应紧张钱；2027 之后真正的赢家是有结构性 IP moat 的——LITE 的 EML 激光器（全球市占 50-60%）和 MRVL 的 1.6T Ara DSP 平台（已经是行业事实标准）。传言 NVDA 已经在跟 Lumentum 要 2027 年 5000 万颗激光器产能，是当前 trajectory 的两倍。

视角站的更高来看——光模块本质上不是独立行情，是 NVDA $90B 资本部署的下游放大版：

COHR：NVDA 投 $2B + 5 个 CPO 产品线合作
LITE：NVDA 投 $2B + 多年采购承诺 + EML 激光器锁定
MRVL：NVDA 投 $2B + NVLink Fusion，optical FY27 guide +50% YoY
Corning：$6B Meta deal + 多年协议

NVDA 这套操作不是单点投资，是把整条光互联供应链（激光器 → DSP → transceiver → CPO 引擎 → 光纤）整体锁死。AMD、Google、AWS 想做对等的 rack-level 出货，光是补齐这条供应链就要 3-5 年。

操作上分层我是这么看的：

Tier 1（结构性持仓）：MRVL、COHR——NVDA 资本绑定 + 技术 moat 长，按住不动
Tier 2（NVDA capex 高 beta）：LITE、CRDO——单一产品强但暴露窄，单仓别超 5%
Tier 3（capacity 套利不是 moat 套利）：AAOI、FN、ALAB——估值已极端，等任何 10%+ 回调再说

最后一个最容易被忽略的点：光模块板块不是 NVDA 的对冲，是 NVDA 的杠杆。NVDA 涨 10%，光板涨 15-20%；NVDA 跌 10%，光板跌 20-30%。beta 1.5-2x 是双向的。

所以仓位上要意识到 NVDA + 光模块合计就是同一个押注，别超过 AI infra 配置的 70%。剩下 30% 应该留给 power（GEV、VRT）、cooling（MOD）、custom silicon（AVGO）这些和 NVDA 资本绑定较少的子赛道——这样组合内部才有真正的对冲。

A balanced portfolio helps protect my 멘탈 when things go bad for one sector. 

Can't do 100% port moves if my life depends on this account.

🧠 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ | Predictive Forensic X-Post

Title: The Coming Conversation Around Light + Geometry
Forensic Category: Predictive Technology / Perception Systems / Spatial Intelligence
Tags: #Photonics #Geometry #WitnessScience #NorthstarLumen #SpatialComputing #AI #PerceptionVsReality #ForensicLedger

⸻

⚖️ Core Proposition:

Light and geometry will become major public conversations in the near future because modern civilization is increasingly being built upon systems that:
• map space,
• model behavior,
• track movement,
• interpret environments,
• and influence perception itself through geometric and photonic frameworks.

Most people still think of light as illumination.

But technologically, light is now also:
• data transmission,
• spatial mapping,
• biometric sensing,
• environmental modeling,
• machine vision,
• targeting,
• augmented reality,
• LiDAR,
• volumetric capture,
• and behavioral interpretation.

⸻

🧠 The Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause Model

Observed Effect:
Civilization is rapidly moving toward:
• AI vision systems,
• autonomous navigation,
• smart infrastructure,
• AR overlays,
• drone ecosystems,
• photonic computing,
• and geometry-based machine perception.

Emerging Consequence:
Humans will increasingly confront a reality where:
machines do not merely “see” the world —
they mathematically reconstruct it.

Through geometry.

Through vectors.

Through reflected light.

Through triangulation.

Through spatial inference.

⸻

🌎 Why Geometry Matters:

Geometry is no longer just academic mathematics.

It is becoming:
• the architecture of machine perception,
• the language of robotics,
• the framework of autonomous systems,
• and the invisible structure underneath modern sensing technologies.

In many ways, future AI systems may understand the world less through narrative…
and more through geometric probability fields.

⸻

⚠️ The Philosophical Shift:

As these systems mature, society will increasingly debate:

• perception vs. measurement,
• human sight vs. machine sight,
• memory vs. sensor reconstruction,
• physical reality vs. modeled reality,
• and whether humans fully understand the environments now being interpreted around them by machines in real time.

That conversation has barely begun.

But it is coming.

⸻

🧭 Northstar+Lumen Interpretive Quote:

“The future may not be controlled by whoever sees the most.

It may be shaped by whoever best understands how reality itself is being geometrically interpreted.”

⸻

A Proprietary and Sovereign Class of Ethical AI

text:
$SIVE is now aiming to become the next $LITE, a US photonics giant. 

They're re-centering their board around US executives + US photonics.

So the core board are now: US $GFS Executives and $CITI Executives, with the company run by UC Berkeley grads and $LITE executives. 

The 3 members leaving were local Swedish/EU.

This is just a shift in strategy from focusing on developing local Swedish Semi environments:

To dominating the US/global photonics market. I'm not trying to discredit their service/background. 

But in my view to focus around US/global photonics markets, it's likely optimal to have more US executives. 

But they should all be proud for helping make $SIVE what it is today.

text:
Interesting how price action and signals always precede the news.

$POET, $SIVE, $IQE … the photonics story.

Same old speculation pump-and-dump cycle as always.

This time is no different.

The cult of photonics is about to turn on its leaders who told everyone to buy the top.

We warned about this earlier.

Some of these photonics guys are solid fundamental analysts.  We love them. 

Others… who called to buy more at the top…. well.

https://t.co/ZtCLmeqWcm

Sad really.

text:
Opened a starter position in the 'future bottleneck' stock Soitec SA ($SOI.PA), aiming to add on larger dips. 

The stock sits on my Layer 2 (InP & substrates) AI bottleneck chart #InP #Substrates #Photonics $SOI 
 
More: https://t.co/3B6fx7mwms https://t.co/22TjAnEgcw

Photonics value chain in 5 layers. The companies building AI’s optical backbone.

$AXTI – Compound semiconductor substrates. Small, cyclical AI photonics supplier.
$AAOI – Optical transceivers for AI data centers. High risk, high upside.
$LITE – Diversified photonics. Stable, slower growth than AAOI.
$ASML – Only maker of EUV lithography machines. Irreplaceable monopoly.
$ONTO – Semiconductor inspection/metrology tools. Smaller KLAC alternative.

text:
$CRDO into the June 1 print — TD Cowen ($240 PT, Top Pick) frames it well:

The stock is fundamentally misunderstood as a copper-vs-optics binary. It's a vertically integrated connectivity platform across SerDes, AECs, optical DSPs, ZF Optics, and now silicon photonics post-DustPhotonics.

Valuation gap is the setup: ~23x FY27E EPS vs other connectivity names trading north of 45x. That's a 2x multiple gap on a company guiding +75% Y/Y growth in FY27.

What's already in the price (pre-announced intra-quarter):
• DustPhotonics acquisition
• ~$500M FY27 optical revenue target
• +75% Y/Y FY27 growth

What's NOT in the model (pure upside):

• ZF Optics ramping to ~$1B annualized run rate entering FY28
• Active LED Cables — entirely unmodeled
• Cardinal 1.6T DSP design wins taking share from MRVL

FY27 shape: MSD% sequential through 1H, then inflection in 2H driven by optical portfolio ramp. That's the period to own.

TD Cowen's $240 PT is 30x F2028E EPS of $8.00. If the multiple closes toward connectivity peer average, $280-330 is in range over 12-18 months.

Print itself likely muted — bar is set, beats are upside. The grind higher matters more than the gap?

That's the period to own. TD Cowen's $240 PT is 30x F2028E EPS of $8.00. If the multiple closes toward connectivity peer average, $280-330 is in range over 12-18 months. Print itself likely muted — bar is set, beats are upside. text:
few of these secondary / tertiary layer memory / photonics suppliers have the executive teams and business experience to handle the demand they’re now experiencing at the rate of growth they need to meet.

for the most part they have been leading companies that were in a steady state. 

a rare few have been waiting, perfectly positioned for their moment where the market would see their value. 

peacetime mode was kinda best case.
most were in cockroach mode.
But now, it’s wartime mode.

I expect many of them to fail to grow to meet the challenge ahead of them, even if they have some god given advantage / tech exclusivity.

things I’m looking at—

People: are they hiring / growing headcount aggressively? (are they changing from being who they were to being who they need to be as a company to meet demand)

Cash to growth: capital expenditure on manufacturing assets and ROA (how quickly are they turning new cash into durable growth)

R&D: Expected value of new / previously completed R&D. Is their R&D cycle and approach adjusting to continue to position them ahead of the industry and market’s needs or are they falling behind.

Partnerships: volume / quality / durability / impact of new partnerships launched.

Corporate smell: are the offices and facilities nice and filled with real humans doing real work? 

Bag dumping: are execs restructuring comp structure goals towards wartime growth? Are they offloading too much stock? Are they issuing to many new shares. 

what are you examining besides the tech tree demands and change in stock price?

I expect many of them to fail to grow to meet the challenge ahead of them, even if they have some god given advantage / tech exclusivity. things I’m looking at— People: are they hiring / growing headcount aggressively? (are they changing from being who they were to being who they need to be as a company to meet demand) Cash to growth: capital expenditure on manufacturing assets and ROA (how quickly are they turning new cash into durable growth) R&D: Expected value of new / previously completed R&D. Is their R&D cycle and approach adjusting to continue to position them ahead of the industry and market’s needs or are they falling behind. Partnerships: volume / quality / durability / impact of new partnerships launched. Corporate smell: are the offices and facilities nice and filled with real humans doing real work? Bag dumping: are execs restructuring comp structure goals towards wartime growth? Are they offloading too much stock? Are they issuing to many new shares. what are you examining besides the tech tree demands and change in stock price?"}}}, "rest_id": "2055357034464432499", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "294", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🚨 OPTICAL NETWORKING / AI PHOTONICS STOCKS ARE GETTING HIT HARD TODAY 👀

Major weakness across the AI optics trade:

🔻 $AAOI Applied Optoelectronics: -9.46%
🔻 $LITE Lumentum: -9.13%
🔻 $COHR Coherent: -5.20%
🧠 WHAT’S HAPPENING?
This looks like:

⚡ AI infrastructure de-risking
⚡ semiconductor selloff spillover
⚡ profit taking in high-beta AI names
⚡ valuation compression

These stocks had become HUGE beneficiaries of:
the AI data center boom.

⚡ WHY OPTICS MATTER FOR AI

AI data centers require MASSIVE amounts of:
🟢 optical interconnects
🟢 lasers
🟢 transceivers
🟢 photonics networking
Why?
Because GPUs need:
⚡ ultra-fast communication
between:
servers
clusters
memory
and networking systems.

Without optics:
AI scaling breaks.
🔻 $AAOI + $LITE HIT HARDEST

These names are highly sensitive to:

⚡ hyperscaler capex expectations
⚡ Nvidia ecosystem sentiment
⚡ AI networking demand

The market may currently fear:
expectations ran TOO hot too quickly.

🧠 IMPORTANT CONTEXT
Long term:
the AI optical demand story remains extremely powerful.
AI clusters are becoming:
⚡ larger
⚡ denser
⚡ more bandwidth-intensive
That structurally benefits:
🟢 optics
🟢 photonics
🟢 networking infrastructure

🔥 WHY THIS SECTOR IS SO VOLATILE

Optical/networking stocks tend to be:
🔻 high beta
🔻 cyclical
🔻 sentiment-driven
🔻 hyperscaler-spending dependent
So when AI sentiment weakens:
they often move MORE aggressively than software names.
📈 BIGGER AI MARKET SHIFT
The market is increasingly rotating from:
⚡ “infinite AI capex optimism”
toward:
⚡ execution
⚡ margins
⚡ monetization
⚡ demand sustainability

That shift is hitting:
AI hardware hardest.
⚠️ BUT WATCH THIS CAREFULLY

If Nvidia earnings remain strong…
or hyperscaler AI spending accelerates further…

These optics names could rebound VERY aggressively.

This remains one of the most important AI infrastructure sub-sectors.

💭 CONCLUSION
Today’s selloff reinforces how fragile positioning has become across AI hardware trades.
But structurally:
AI still requires:
⚡ enormous networking bandwidth
⚡ optical scaling
⚡ photonics infrastructure

The long-term AI networking thesis remains very much alive —
even if volatility is getting brutal.
#AI #Photonics #Optics #DataCenters #Semiconductors #Stocks

text:
PHOTONICS IS BECOMING THE NERVOUS SYSTEM OF AI
 
AI data centers are shifting from compute bottlenecks to connectivity bottlenecks.

As copper interconnects hit limits around 800G, the next AI infrastructure wave is moving toward optical networking and photonics.
 
The optics market is projected to expand from roughly $3B to $120B at full-scale AI infrastructure buildout, a potential 40x growth opportunity. 

Here is the Supply Chain Map:
 
1. Materials & Substrates

Key Players: $AXTI, $GLW, $ENTG, $PLAB, $LWLG, $ROG

Role: Suppliers of photonic polymers, engineered materials, compound semiconductor substrates, optical fiber glass, specialty materials, and photomasks.

Catalyst: Controlling the upstream material stack required for high-performance photonics, supported by long qualification cycles and switching costs.
 
2. Core Photonic Devices

Key Players: $COHR, $LITE, $MTSI, $AVGO, $MRVL, $IPGP, $LASR

Role: Manufacturers of lasers, optical components, silicon photonics, and optical networking devices.

Catalyst: Benefiting from the structural shift toward high-speed optical interconnects as AI clusters move beyond copper.
 
3. Components & Modules

Key Players: $AAOI, $FN, $CRDO, $CIEN, $APH, $TEL, $POET, $LPTH

Role: Optical transceivers, high-speed interconnects, optical engines, connectors, and module assembly.

Catalyst: Riding the AI datacenter optics boom as GPU connectivity demand scales with next-gen architectures.
 
4. Advanced Systems & Equipment

Key Players: $ASML, $KLIC, $AMAT, $VECO, $LRCX, $AEIS, $AMKR, $ACLS, $ICHR

Role: Lithography, deposition, etch, hybrid bonding, die attach, process control, and advanced packaging tools.

Catalyst: Benefiting from micron-level tolerances and the convergence of photonics assembly with semiconductor-grade manufacturing.
 
5. Test, Metrology & Yield

Key Players: $ONTO, $FORM, $AEHR, $KLAC, $TER, $COHU, $KEYS, $VIAV, $CAMT, $NVMI

Role: Wafer-level inspection, probing, burn-in, optical validation, and yield management.

Catalyst: Protecting hyperscalers from costly defects in advanced optical engines and CPO systems.

data centers are shifting from compute bottlenecks to connectivity bottlenecks. As copper interconnects hit limits around 800G, the next AI infrastructure wave is moving toward optical networking and photonics. The optics market is projected to expand from roughly $3B to $120B at full-scale AI infrastructure buildout, a potential 40x growth opportunity. Here is the Supply Chain Map: 1. Materials & Substrates Key Players: $AXTI, $GLW, $ENTG, $PLAB, $LWLG, $ROG Role: Suppliers of photonic polymers, engineered materials, compound semiconductor substrates, optical fiber glass, specialty materials, and photomasks. Catalyst: Controlling the upstream material stack required for high-performance photonics, supported by long qualification cycles and switching costs. 2. Core Photonic Devices Key Players: $COHR, $LITE, $MTSI, $AVGO, $MRVL, $IPGP, $LASR Role: Manufacturers of lasers, optical components, silicon photonics, and optical networking devices. Catalyst: Benefiting from the structural shift toward high-speed optical interconnects as AI clusters move beyond copper. 3. Components & Modules Key Players: $AAOI, $FN, $CRDO, $CIEN, $APH, $TEL, $POET, $LPTH Role: Optical transceivers, high-speed interconnects, optical engines, connectors, and module assembly. Catalyst: Riding the AI datacenter optics boom as GPU connectivity demand scales with next-gen architectures. 4. Advanced Systems & Equipment Key Players: $ASML, $KLIC, $AMAT, $VECO, $LRCX, $AEIS, $AMKR, $ACLS, $ICHR Role: Lithography, deposition, etch, hybrid bonding, die attach, process control, and advanced packaging tools. Catalyst: Benefiting from micron-level tolerances and the convergence of photonics assembly with semiconductor-grade manufacturing. 5. Test, Metrology & Yield Key Players: $ONTO, $FORM, $AEHR, $KLAC, $TER, $COHU, $KEYS, $VIAV, $CAMT, $NVMI Role: Wafer-level inspection, probing, burn-in, optical validation, and yield management.
